title: 6 takeaways from the U.S. World Cup win over Bosnia-Herzegovina
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. men's soccer team beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 in the World Cup knockout round in San Francisco.
Folarin Balogun scored but was later sent off with a controversial red card, missing the upcoming Belgium match.
Malik Tillman's late free kick secured the win, giving the U.S. its first knockout round victory since 2002.

title: Brighton man charged in 2025 killing of 15-year-old in Allston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Armani Collazos, 26, charged with murder and multiple firearm offenses in 2025 shooting of a 15-year-old in Allston.
Collazos pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; victim Phillip Franco died from gunshot wounds in local hospital.
Miranda Bodden, 22, faces five accessory charges and was released on $2,000 bail; next court date set for August 8.

title: Bus driver strike disrupts bus service in Merrimack Valley
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Merrimack Valley bus service halted as MeVa workers strike over proposed weekend shift changes.
The strike involves Teamsters Local 170 members, opposing a pay restructure affecting overtime rates and weekend shifts.
MeVa management offers a 25% pay raise over three years, aiming to improve weekend coverage due to rider demand.

title: Charania: Jaylen Brown ‘never requested a trade’ from Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers by the Boston Celtics without requesting a trade.
The Celtics traded Brown after unsuccessfully attempting to acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo with an offer including Brown.
Despite Brown's desire to stay, Celtics management decided to trade him, acquiring Paul George and draft picks in return.

title: 2 men involved in 2015 Dorchester home invasion, murder sentenced after conviction, guilty plea
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Victor Arrington sentenced to life in prison for 2015 Dorchester home invasion murder, plus 42-45 years for other charges.
James Boyd, also involved, sentenced to time served with five years probation after pleading guilty to home invasion and kidnapping.
Home invasion victims Richard Long was killed, fiancée survived attack; case used iPhone location data to convict Arrington.

title: What to know about heat warnings, cooling centers in Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu issued an excessive heat warning for Boston through Friday, with a heat index expected up to 112 degrees.
Boston activated cooling centers, including BCYF community centers, to offer relief from the heat, especially on Friday.
Boston Public Health Commission provides 24-hour emergency shelters for homeless individuals during this excessive heat period.

title: Dartmouth Mall rolls out new limits on youth visiting mall without an adult
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dartmouth Mall's new policy requires those under 18 to be with an adult after 5 p.m. on weekends.
The policy aims to ensure a secure environment and applies to all mall areas, excluding stores with exterior entrances.
Youth employees can still work unsupervised, but must provide proof of employment and go directly to work.

title: Bruins reportedly acquire defenseman Will Borgen in trade with Rangers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins acquired defenseman Will Borgen from the New York Rangers for a 2027 second-round pick and a 2028 conditional third-round pick.
Borgen, 29, is expected to fill a top-four defensive role after Brandon Carlo's trade to Toronto in March 2025.
Borgen signed a four-year contract with a $4.1 million annual cap hit; he played 75 games last season with the Rangers.

title: Celtics reportedly send Jaylen Brown to 76ers in stunning trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
Brown played 10 seasons with the Celtics, winning NBA Finals MVP in 2024 and earning All-NBA Second Team honors.
Paul George averaged 17.3 points last season; the trade includes two first-round picks for 2028 and 2031.

title: Sewage overflow issue in Merrimack River is resolved, officials say, although some beaches are still closed
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sewage overflow into the Merrimack River from Haverhill was stopped after contractors installed a temporary bypass pipeline.
Despite the overflow being resolved, officials advise avoiding contact with the river near Haverhill and downstream for at least 48 hours.
Several beaches remain closed for swimming due to elevated bacteria levels, but Ipswich has reopened most of its beaches.
